intro: Created on August 15, 1966 by Mayor John Lindsay to consolidate a number of existing human services offices under one umbrella, HRA traces the city's welfare efforts back to the New York Almshouse built in 1736. Today it is the largest local social services agency in the country, administering more than 15 major public assistance programs — including cash assistance, food assistance, and Medicaid — that reach more than three million New Yorkers annually.
